front: Song: Two princes perform this duet while trying to one-up each other over their love for Cinderella and Rapunzel
Song: The singers of this duet imagine 'what's intriguing / or half so fatiguing / as what's out of reach?'
Song: Cinderella consoles Little Red, Jack, and the Baker by singing this song after the deaths of Granny and the Baker's Wife
Song: Cinderella sings about her 'first big decision' — whether to abandon her slipper on some steps — in this number
back: "On the Steps of the Palace" (Into the Woods)
work: Songs: "On the Steps of the Palace"

front: Song: The Witch implores Rapunzel: 'Stay with me / The world is dark and wild. / Stay a child while you can be a child'
Song: The Baker's Wife briefly entertains an affair with Cinderella's Prince during this duet, after which she is crushed by a giant's foot
Musical: Joanna Gleason originated this role, collecting a cow, a cape, some hair, and a slipper to have a baby
work: Characters: The Baker and Baker's Wife

front: Musical: This character opens and closes Into the Woods by singing 'I wish'
Musical: At the end of Into the Woods, this character breaks up with the Prince over his infidelity and helps raise Little Red and Jack with the Baker
Musical: This character calls birds to help her pick lentils out of ashes so she can go to the King's Festival
Musical: Anna Kendrick played this character in the 2014 film adaptation of Into the Woods
Musical: A motif in this show begins with a descending fifth (A down to D, E, C-sharp, B), associated with the warning 'Don't you know what's out there in the world?'
Musical: The motif 'short D, long E' represents the closing words of this show
Musical: This show was Sondheim's second collaboration with librettist James Lapine, after Sunday in the Park with George
Musical: In the stage version of Into the Woods, the narrator is sacrificed to one of these beings. Jack sings about encountering these 'big, tall, terrible' beings.
Musical: Five of these items were traded for Milky White; a character asks 'Were they worthless? Were they oversold?'
Musical: This character needs a cow as white as milk, a cape as red as blood, hair as yellow as corn, and a slipper as pure as gold to reverse a curse
Musical: This character feeds Milky White golden cornsilk to fulfill a prophecy about 'hair as yellow as corn'
